UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's profile within the application, linked to their authentication ID but not storing credentials.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity, typically matching the external authentication system's user ID.

email.description: The email address associated with the user profile.

name.description: The display name of the user.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was last updated.

Property.title: Property
Property.description: Represents a rental property managed by a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Property entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who owns or manages this property. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Property)

address.description: The full postal address of the property.

propertyType.description: The type of property (e.g., Flat, House, Bungalow, Maisonette, Studio, HMO).

status.description: The current occupancy status of the property (e.g., Vacant, Occupied, Under Maintenance).

numberOfBedrooms.description: The number of bedrooms in the property.

numberOfBathrooms.description: The number of bathrooms in the property.

imageUrl.description: URL to an image of the property, if uploaded.

notes.description: General notes or additional details about the property.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the property record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the property record was last updated.

Tenant.title: Tenant
Tenant.description: Stores details for a tenant associated with a specific rental property.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Tenant entity.

propertyId.description: Reference to the Property this tenant is renting. (Relationship: Property 1:N Tenant)

name.description: The full name of the tenant.

email.description: The email address of the tenant.

phoneNumber.description: The telephone number of the tenant.

tenancyStartDate.description: The start date of the tenancy agreement.

tenancyEndDate.description: The projected end date of the tenancy agreement.

notes.description: Any additional notes or specific details about the tenant or tenancy.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the tenant record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the tenant record was last updated.

MaintenanceRequest.title: MaintenanceRequest
MaintenanceRequest.description: Records maintenance issues reported for a property.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MaintenanceRequest entity.

propertyId.description: Reference to the Property the maintenance request is for. (Relationship: Property 1:N MaintenanceRequest)

description.description: A detailed description of the maintenance issue.

urgencyLevel.description: The urgency level of the maintenance request (e.g., Low, Medium, High, Critical).

status.description: The current status of the maintenance request (e.g., Open, In Progress, Completed, Pending, Cancelled).

reportedDate.description: Timestamp when the maintenance request was reported.

completedDate.description: Timestamp when the maintenance request was completed, if applicable.

notes.description: Internal notes regarding the maintenance task or resolution.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the maintenance request record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the maintenance request record was last updated.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Inspection entity.

propertyId.description: Reference to the Property where the inspection is to take place. (Relationship: Property 1:N Inspection)

scheduledDate.description: The scheduled date and time for the inspection.

inspectionType.description: The type of inspection (e.g., Move-In, Routine, Move-Out, Ad-hoc).

status.description: The current status of the inspection (e.g., Scheduled, Completed, Cancelled).

notes.description: Any notes or specific instructions for the inspection.

reportUrl.description: URL to the inspection report, if available.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the inspection record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the inspection record was last updated.

Document.title: Document
Document.description: Stores metadata about documents uploaded for properties.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Document entity.

propertyId.description: Reference to the Property this document is associated with. (Relationship: Property 1:N Document)

documentType.description: The type of document (e.g., Tenancy Agreement, Safety Certificate, Invoice, Other).

fileName.description: The original file name of the uploaded document.

fileUrl.description: The URL where the document is stored (e.g., cloud storage URL).

uploadDate.description: Timestamp when the document was uploaded.

description.description: A brief description or summary of the document's content.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the document record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the document record was last updated.

ActivityLog.title: ActivityLog
ActivityLog.description: Logs significant activities and events within the application related to users and properties.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ActivityLog entry.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who performed the activity. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N ActivityLog)

propertyId.description: Reference to the Property related to this activity, if applicable. (Relationship: Property 1:N ActivityLog)

eventType.description: The type of event that occurred (e.g., 'PropertyAdded', 'MaintenanceLogged', 'InspectionScheduled', 'DocumentUploaded', 'UserProfileUpdated').

description.description: A detailed, human-readable description of the activity.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the activity log record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the activity log record was last updated.

definition.description: Stores individual user profile information. The document ID `{userId}` MUST match the authenticated user's UID (`request.auth.uid`).

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching `request.auth.uid`.

definition.description: Stores details for rental properties. Each property document MUST include a `userId` field that matches the `{userId}` path segment and `request.auth.uid` for ownership and authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this property.

params.name: propertyId
params.description: The unique identifier for the property.

definition.description: Stores tenant details associated with a specific property. Each tenant document MUST include a denormalized `userId` field that matches the `{userId}` path segment (the property owner) for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent property.

params.name: propertyId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent property.

params.name: tenantId
params.description: The unique identifier for the tenant.

definition.description: Records maintenance issues reported for a property. Each maintenance request document MUST include a denormalized `userId` field that matches the `{userId}` path segment (the property owner) for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent property.

params.name: propertyId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent property.

params.name: maintenanceRequestId
params.description: The unique identifier for the maintenance request.

definition.description: Manages scheduled inspections for properties. Each inspection document MUST include a denormalized `userId` field that matches the `{userId}` path segment (the property owner) for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent property.

params.name: propertyId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent property.

params.name: inspectionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the inspection.

definition.description: Stores metadata about documents uploaded for properties. Each document MUST include a denormalized `userId` field that matches the `{userId}` path segment (the property owner) for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent property.

params.name: propertyId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent property.

params.name: documentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the document.

definition.description: Logs significant activities and events within the application. Each activity log document MUST include a `userId` field that matches the `{userId}` path segment and `request.auth.uid` for ownership.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who performed the activity.

params.name: activityLogId
params.description: The unique identifier for the activity log entry.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure is designed following a strict hierarchical pattern, prioritizing user ownership and data privacy. This design ensures robust security rules, scalability, and simplified debugging by adhering to core principles.

**Authorization Independence:** This is achieved by embedding user ownership directly into the document paths and denormalizing the owner's `userId` into all subcollection documents. For instance, `Property` documents are stored under `/userProfiles/{userId}/properties/{propertyId}`, where `{userId}` directly corresponds to `request.auth.uid`. For subcollections like `Tenants`, `MaintenanceRequests`, `Inspections`, and `Documents`, the `userId` of the property owner (which matches the `{userId}` path segment) **MUST** be explicitly included as a field within each document. This critical denormalization allows security rules to evaluate `resource.data.userId == request.auth.uid` directly on any document within these subcollections, eliminating the need for expensive and complex `get()` calls to parent documents. This prevents authorization dependencies across document boundaries, making operations atomic and resilient to data model changes.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):** The hierarchical structure inherently supports Query-Allowable Patterns. By nesting all user-specific data under `/userProfiles/{userId}/...`, any `list` operation is implicitly scoped to the authenticated user. For example, a query to list `Tenants` for a specific property would be `/userProfiles/{request.auth.uid}/properties/{propertyId}/tenants`. The security rules can then simply check `allow list: if request.auth.uid == userId;` on the `/userProfiles/{userId}/...` path, and further ensure `resource.data.userId == userId` for the denormalized fields. This structure ensures that users can only query their own data, and that all returned documents satisfy the security predicate. The initial `Runtime FirebaseError` for `/tenants` directly illustrates the problem solved by this approach: attempting to `list` from a root-level collection (`/tenants`) without proper ownership context leads to permission errors because the rules cannot efficiently determine ownership across disparate collections.

**Structural Segregation:** All collections defined here (`userProfiles`, `properties`, `tenants`, `maintenanceRequests`, `inspections`, `documents`, `activityLogs`) maintain a homogeneous security posture: they are private to the `userId` in their respective paths. There is no mixing of public and private data within the same collection, which significantly simplifies the security rules and prevents accidental data exposure.

**Access Modeling & Consistency:** Path-based ownership (`/userProfiles/{userId}/...`) is consistently applied across all entities. Wildcards are semantically named (`{userId}`, `{propertyId}`). Explicit state modeling is used via `status` fields. Denormalized `userId` fields enforce ownership across nested collections. This radical consistency makes the entire data model predictable and easy to reason about for both development and security auditing.
